agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond Council to explore funding for student scholarships
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will receive a presentation from Richmond Promise, a local organization that helps Richmond students access and complete post-secondary education (including college, trade school, and university). After the presentation, Council will consider directing staff to explore funding options to support scholarships for Richmond youth. This is a proposed step — no funding has been approved yet.
agenda_briefs.action_required: No action needed from business owners at this time. Watch for future council decisions on scholarship funding.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
Q.2
Presentation from Richmond Promise: Helping to build a stronger Richmond by deeply supporting Richmond students to access and successfully complete post-secondary education (not only 4 year college)
•  RECEIVE a presentation from Richmond Promise on the organization’s current scope of services, how it supports youth before, during, and after post-secondary education, and the impact it has achieved to date and to highlight the program’s current needs as it continues to serve Richmond students; and DIRECT staff to explore funding to support scholarships for Richmond youth - Vice Mayor Doria Robinson (510-620-6568).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond Council to vote on opposing Montezuma CO2 pipeline
agenda_briefs.summary: Richmond City Council is set to vote on a resolution opposing the proposed Montezuma Carbon Sequestration Hub. This project would involve injecting carbon dioxide waste underground and building a large network of pipelines through Richmond, Contra Costa, and Solano Counties. Council members are concerned about the health and safety risks to residents from potential pipeline leaks or accidents. The resolution is up for public comment and council deliberation — it has not been approved yet.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Attend the public comment session or contact Councilmember Claudia Jimenez (510-620-6565) to voice your concerns before the vote.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
Q.1
Opposition to Proposed CO2 Waste Dumping Projects & CO2 Pipelines in Richmond, Contra Costa, and Solano Counties
RECEIVE a presentation on proposed Montezuma CO2 Hub, a carbon waste dumping project, that would require a massive build out of pipelines through Richmond, risking the health and safety of Richmond residents and ADOPT a resolution that opposes the proposed Montezuma Carbon Sequestration Hub's CO2 pipeline waste dumping projects and CO2 pipelines in Richmond, Contra Costa, and Solano Counties - Councilmember Claudia Jimenez (510-620-6565). This item was continued from the April 28, 2026, and May 5, 2026, meeting. THE PRESENTATION FOR THIS ITEM WAS RECEIVED ON MAY 5, 2026. THIS ITEM IS FOR PUBLIC COMMENT AND COUNCIL DELIBERATION ONLY.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond council to hold public hearing on city vacancies
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider holding a public hearing and receiving a presentation on the status of city vacancies and the city's recruitment and retention efforts in 2025. This is a proposed item on the agenda — it has not yet been approved or enacted. The hearing would allow the public to learn about staffing gaps and how the city is trying to fill them.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
P.4
Annual Public Hearing on Vacancies
•  HOLD a public hearing and RECEIVE a presentation on the status of vacancies and the City’s recruitment and retention efforts in 2025 – Human Resources (Nickie Mastay/Sharrone Taylor 510-620-6803).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond council to vote on new Master Fee Schedule
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to consider a proposed update to the city's Master Fee Schedule, which would establish new fees for various city services. A public hearing will be held before council votes on the ordinance (a proposed law) at a first reading. If approved, the new fees could affect the cost of business licences, permits, and other municipal services. The proposal is not yet in effect — it is up for approval at an upcoming council meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Attend the public hearing to voice your concerns or support before council votes on the new fee schedule.

key_dates.label: Public hearing on proposed Master Fee Schedule

agenda_briefs.source_body:
P.3
Master Fee Schedule Update
•  HOLD a public hearing; and INTRODUCE an ordinance (first reading) establishing certain fees for the proposed Master Fee Schedule – Finance Department (Emily Combs/Mubeen Qader/Antonio Banuelos 510-620-6741).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond to levy special assessments for unpaid code fines
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a proposal to add unpaid administrative citation fees and nuisance abatement costs directly onto property tax bills as a 'special assessment'. This means if a property owner owes money for code violations (like overgrown weeds, illegal dumping, or other nuisances) and hasn't paid, the city could put a lien on the property and collect the debt through property taxes. A public hearing will be held before any decision is made. This is not yet approved — it is up for discussion and a vote at a future council meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Attend the public hearing to voice your concerns or support before council votes.

key_dates.label: Public hearing — Richmond special assessment bylaw for unpaid code fines

agenda_briefs.industries: Landlord & Property Mgmt, Construction & Development, Retail
agenda_briefs.source_body:
P.2
Levy of Special Assessments for Unpaid Administrative Citation and Nuisance Abatement Costs
•  HOLD a public hearing; and ADOPT a resolution authorizing the levy of special assessments against certain properties with unpaid invoices related to administrative citation fees, and nuisance abatement costs pursuant to Richmond Municipal Code Chapters 2.62, 6.38, 9.22, and 11.76 – Community Development Department/Code Enforcement Division (Lina Velasco/ Eva Mann 510-621-1279).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond zoning and land-use changes to match state housing law
agenda_briefs.summary: Richmond City Council is set to vote on proposed changes to the city's zoning bylaws and General Plan (the city's long-term land-use blueprint). These changes are meant to bring local rules in line with recent updates to California state housing law, as part of the city's 6th Cycle Housing Element. If approved, the amendments would affect how land can be used and what types of housing can be built in certain areas. A public hearing will be held before the vote.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Attend the public hearing to voice your support or concerns about how these zoning changes could affect your property or business.

key_dates.label: Public hearing on proposed Richmond zoning amendments

agenda_briefs.source_body:
P.1
General Plan and Zoning Text Amendments Related to 6th Cycle Housing Element Program Implementation
•  HOLD a public hearing; INTRODUCE (first reading) an ordinance, as recommended by Richmond’s Planning Commission, amending Articles 15.04.104, 15.04.201, 15.04.202, 15.04.203, 15.04.602, 15.04.607; Sections 15.04.610.180, 15.04.610.360, 15.04.805.040 and 15.04.806.040 of the Richmond Municipal Code (RMC); and ADOPT a resolution, as recommended by Richmond’s Planning Commission, amending the General Plan 2030 Land Use and Urban Design Element, to implement certain programs identified in the City’s adopted 6th Cycle Housing Element to align with recent changes to State Law - Community Development Department (Lina Velasco/Avery Stark 510-620-6714).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond council to review draft 2026-27 budget and capital plan
agenda_briefs.summary: Richmond City Council is set to receive updates on the draft Fiscal Year 2026-27 Annual Operating Budget and the FY 2026-31 Five-Year Capital Improvement Plan. As part of this review, council will hear presentations from the Community Services, Economic Development, and Fire Departments. This is a proposed budget that has not yet been approved — council will discuss and consider it before making a final decision. The budget outlines how the city plans to spend money on services and major projects over the next year and five years.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this budget could affect city services and fees that impact your business.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
O.1
Fiscal Year 2026-27 Draft Budget Department Presentations and Budget Updates
•  RECEIVE an update on departmental presentations from Community Services, Economic Development, and Fire Departments as part of the continued review of the draft Fiscal Year (FY) 2026-27 Annual Operating Budget and FY 2026-31 FiveYear Capital Improvement Plan, with discussions upon completion of all departmental presentations; and RECEIVE updated financial and operational considerations – City Manager’s Office/ Finance Department (Shasa Curl/Emily Combs/Mubeen Qader 510-412-2077).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond sidewalk vendor rules updated — council to vote
agenda_briefs.summary: Richmond City Council is set to vote on proposed changes to the Sidewalk Vendor Ordinance (Chapter 7.42 of the Richmond Municipal Code). The proposed bylaw would update how vendors apply for permits, how waivers (exceptions to the rules) are handled, and how the city enforces the rules. This item was first discussed at council meetings on April 28 and May 5, 2026, and has been brought back for a final decision. If approved, the new rules would affect anyone who sells goods from a sidewalk stall, cart, or table in Richmond.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if passed, these changes could affect your sidewalk vending permit and how you operate.

key_dates.label: Richmond sidewalk vendor bylaw — council vote date

agenda_briefs.industries: Food Service, Retail, Grocery & Food Retail
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.13.f
Introduce Ordinance Amending Sidewalk Vendor Regulations (RMC Chapter 7.42)
INTRODUCE an ordinance (first reading) amending Chapter 7.42 of the Richmond Municipal Code (Sidewalk Vendor Ordinance) to update administrative procedures, waiver provisions, and enforcement mechanisms - Public Works Department (Andy Cho 620-6547/Robert Armijo 620-5477). This item was continued from the April 28, 2026, and May 5, 2026, meeting.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: $390,000 contract amendment for Richmond electric bikeshare
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed third contract amendment with Charleston Mobility for up to $390,000 to keep the citywide electric bikeshare program running until the end of the fiscal year. This short-term funding would give the city time to decide the program's long-term future during the Fiscal Year 2026-27 budget process. The money would come from the Environmental and Community Investment Agreement (ECIA) Transportation budget. This item was previously discussed at the April 28 and May 5, 2026 meetings and is now up for a decision.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if approved, the bikeshare program will continue operating; if not, it may end soon.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.13.e
Contract Amendment No. 3 with Charleston Mobility for Electric Bikeshare Operations
APPROVE a third contract amendment with Charleston Mobility in an amount not to exceed $390,000 for a total not-to-exceed contract amount of $3,540,341, to support the continued operation of the citywide electric bikeshare program till the end of the fiscal year to allow time to determine the future of the program during the Fiscal Year 2026-27 budget process; and APPROPRIATE $390,000 from the Environmental and Community Investment Agreement (ECIA) Transportation budget to cover the cost of the contract amendment – Public Works Department/Community Services Department (Gabino Arredondo 510-620-6606/LaShonda White 510-620-6828).​ This item was continued from the April 28, 2026, and May 5, 2026, meeting.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: $1.28M slope stabilization project on Santa Fe Avenue
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a proposal to fund a slope stabilization project on Santa Fe Avenue. The plan would move $72,623.62 from another road project, take $205,502.81 from a different transportation project, and pull $1,000,000 from the city's insurance reserve fund. The total of about $1.28 million would pay for design work, geotechnical services, and construction. This is a proposed decision — it has not been approved yet.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.13.d
Appropriate Funds for the Santa Fe Avenue Slope Stabilization Project
•  ADOPT a resolution to RATIFY the transfer of $72,623.62 from the Santa Rita Road Stabilization Project; APPROPRIATE $205,502.81 from the Quick-Build project funded through California’s Active Transportation Program for Cutting/Hoffman Project to fund design and geotechnical services; and APPROPRIATE $1,000,000 from the Insurance Reserve Fund to the General Capital Fund in the Fiscal Year 2026–27 budget for construction of the Santa Fe Avenue Slope Stabilization Project - Public Works Department (Hillal Hamdan 510-621-1612/ Daniel Chavarria 510-620-5478).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond council to vote on $3.25M road repair fund list
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a resolution that would approve a list of road maintenance and rehabilitation projects for the 2026–27 fiscal year. If passed, the decision would also set aside $3,251,983 from the state's Senate Bill 1 Road Maintenance and Rehabilitation Account (RMRA) — a special fund for fixing roads — into a dedicated city account for the Public Works Department. This is a proposed funding plan, not yet approved.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.13.c
Fiscal Year 2026–27 Senate Bill 1 Road Maintenance and Rehabilitation Fund Project List
•  ADOPT a resolution approving the Fiscal Year 2026–27 Senate Bill (SB) 1 Road Maintenance and Rehabilitation Account (RMRA) project list and APPROPRIATE $3,251,983 in RMRA funds to SB 1 Fund 1019 - Public Works Department (Hillal Hamdan 510-621-1612/Robert Armijo 510-620-5477).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond Council to vote on Complete Streets funding plan
agenda_briefs.summary: Richmond Council is set to vote on a proposed funding allocation plan for Complete Streets improvements. This plan would direct money toward pavement management, streetscape upgrades, and other street-related projects. The decision has not been made yet — it is up for approval at an upcoming council meeting. If approved, the plan would affect how public works funds are spent on local roads and sidewalks.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council meeting date and attend or submit comments if you want to have a say on how street improvement funds are spent.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.13.b
Complete Streets Improvement Elements Project Funding Allocation Plan
•  APPROVE the Complete Streets Improvements (Pavement Management, Streetscape Improvements, etc.) funding allocation plan – Public Works Department (Josef Munoz 510-620-6671/Daniel Chavarria 510-620-5478).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Council to accept Lincoln Elementary safe routes project as complete
agenda_briefs.summary: Richmond City Council is set to vote on a resolution to formally accept the Lincoln Elementary School Safe Routes to School Improvement Project as complete. If approved, this would authorize the city clerk to ratify the previously recorded Notice of Completion (a formal document stating the project is finished) and authorize the city engineer to release any remaining bonds and retention money (funds held back to ensure the work was done properly). This is a routine step to close out the project, but it signals that the safety improvements around the school are now in place.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.13.a
Acceptance of the Lincoln Elementary School Safe Routes to School Improvement Project
•  ADOPT a resolution to ACCEPT the Lincoln Elementary School Safe Routes to School Improvement Project as complete; AUTHORIZE the city clerk to ratify the previously recorded Notice of Completion; and AUTHORIZE the city engineer to execute and release applicable bonds and retention – Public Works Department (Hillal Hamdan 621-1612/Daniel Chavarria 620-5478).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond council to vote on $7.2M animal control contract
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed contract with the Contra Costa County Animal Services Department worth up to $7.2 million. This contract would continue animal control services for the 2026–2027 fiscal year, with an option to extend for three more years through June 2030. The decision was postponed from a previous meeting and is now up for approval at the next council meeting. If approved, the contract would cover the period from July 1, 2022, to June 30, 2030.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this contract could affect animal control fees or services for your business.

key_dates.label: Council vote on animal control contract — Richmond

agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.12.c
Agreement with Contra Costa County Animal Services Department for Ongoing Animal Control Services
•  APPROVE and AUTHORIZE a contract with Contra Costa County Animal Services Department in an amount not to exceed $7,200,000 for the continuation of Animal Control Services for Fiscal Year 2026–2027, with an additional three-year term for Fiscal Years 2027–2028, 2028–2029, and 2029–2030, for a total contract term of July 1, 2022, through June 30, 2030 – Police Department (Chief Timothy Simmons 510-621-1802). This item was continued from the May 5, 2026, meeting.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond council to vote on $75,000 shooting range rental contract
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed three-year contract with the Richmond Rod and Gun Club for shooting range facility rentals. If approved, the contract would cost the city up to $25,000 per year, for a total not to exceed $75,000, running from July 1, 2026 through June 30, 2029. The contract also includes two optional one-year extensions at the same annual rate. This is a 'sole-source' contract, meaning the city would hire this club without seeking bids from other companies. The Police Department is recommending this deal for training and other law enforcement needs.
agenda_briefs.action_required: No action needed from business owners — this is an internal city contract for police training.

key_dates.label: Council vote on proposed shooting range contract

agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.12.b
Contract with Richmond Rod and Gun Club for Shooting Range Facility Rental
•  APPROVE a three-year sole-source contract with Richmond Rod and Gun Club for shooting range facility rentals in an amount not to exceed $25,000 annually, for a total not to exceed $75,000, for the term July 1, 2026, through June 30, 2029, with two one-year extension options at $25,000 per year – Police Department (Chief Timothy Simmons 510-621-1802).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: $150,000 GPS contract for Richmond police vehicles
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider approving a three-year contract with Verizon Connect to provide GPS tracking and management for police vehicles. The contract would cost up to $150,000 from July 1, 2026 to June 30, 2029, with two optional one-year extensions at up to $50,000 per year. This is a proposed decision — it has not been approved yet.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.12.a
Contract with Verizon Connect for Police Vehicle GPS Service
•  APPROVE a contract with Verizon Connect for monthly GPS tracking and management in an amount not to exceed $150,000 over a three-year term from July 1, 2026, to June 30, 2029, plus two one-year extension options not to exceed $50,000 per year – Police Department (Chief Timothy Simmons 510-621-1802).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond council to add 4 new seats to Macdonald Avenue task force
agenda_briefs.summary: Richmond Council is set to vote on adding four new stakeholder positions to the Macdonald Avenue Corridor Task Force. If approved, these seats would be filled through the city's standard boards and commissions process. This means the task force — which advises on planning and development along Macdonald Avenue — would get more voices from the community. The proposal is up for approval at an upcoming council meeting and has not been decided yet.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if approved, you may apply for a stakeholder seat through the city's boards and commissions process.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.11.e
Expansion of the Macdonald Avenue Corridor Task Force Membership
•  APPROVE the addition of four (4) new stakeholder positions to the Macdonald Avenue Corridor Task Force which will be filled through the standard boards and commissions process—Mayor’s Office (Eduardo Martinez (510-620-6502).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond council committee to rewrite council rules
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a request to create a special committee (called an ad-hoc committee) to rewrite the City Council Rules of Procedure and Order. If approved, the mayor would appoint members to this committee, who would then work with city staff to draft proposed changes. The goal is to make council meetings run more smoothly and efficiently. The full council would vote on any proposed rule changes within 60 days after the committee is formed. This is a proposal only — no rules have been changed yet.
agenda_briefs.action_required: No action needed from business owners at this time. Watch for future council decisions on any rule changes that could affect how public hearings or licensing matters are handled.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.10.a
Ad-Hoc Committee to Amend City Council Rules of Procedure and Order
•  REQUEST that Mayor Eduardo Martinez appoint a City Council ad hoc committee to work with the city clerk, city attorney, and city manager to develop proposed amendments to the City Council Rules of Procedure and Order and other operational refinements to improve efficiency, for consideration by the full City Council within 60 days of the committee’s appointment - City Clerk’s Office/City Attorney’s Office/City Manager’s Office (Pamela Christian 510-620-6513/Shannon Moore 510-620-6509/Shasa Curl 510-620-6512).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: 5% salary adjustment for Richmond city clerk in 2026
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider approving a 5% salary increase for the city clerk, effective March 2, 2026. This proposed adjustment follows the terms of the IFPTE Local 21 Executive Management Memorandum of Understanding (a formal agreement between the city and a union representing senior managers). The decision has not been made yet — it is up for approval at an upcoming council meeting.

key_dates.label: Proposed city clerk salary adjustment takes effect

agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.9.a
Annual Compensation Adjustment for the City Clerk
•  APPROVE a five percent (5%) salary adjustment for the city clerk effective March 2, 2026, in accordance with the IFPTE Local 21 Executive Management Memorandum of Understanding Section 6.4D – Human Resources Department (Nickie Mastay/Sharrone Taylor 510-620-6602).

agenda_briefs.title: Council to vote on wildfire preparedness committee recommendations
agenda_briefs.summary: Richmond City Council is set to consider recommendations from the Wildfire Preparedness Ad Hoc Committee to improve the city's wildfire preparedness and mitigation efforts. Council will provide direction to staff on which recommendation to pursue during the budget process. The committee will be disbanded after presenting its findings. This is a proposed decision — it has not been approved yet.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if approved, new wildfire rules or programs could affect your property or business operations.
agenda_briefs.industries: Landlord & Property Mgmt, Construction & Development, Professional Services
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.8.c
Wildfire Preparedness Ad Hoc Committee Recommendations
•  RECEIVE a written presentation from the Wildfire Preparedness Ad Hoc Committee; PROVIDE DIRECTION to staff on the City Council’s preferred recommendation to improve the City’s wildfire preparedness and mitigation efforts during the budget checklist on proposed appropriations; and ACKNOWLEDGE the disbanding of the Wildfire Preparedness Ad Hoc Committee – Wildfire Preparedness Ad Hoc Committee (Chief Aaron Osorio 510-307-8021).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond council to vote on extending EMS agreement with Contra Costa County
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed amendment to the city's interagency agreement with Contra Costa County Health Services for the Emergency Medical Services (EMS) Program. If approved, the agreement would be extended through June 30, 2027, and an additional $229,462 in Measure H funding would be accepted, bringing the total contract payment limit to $458,924. This is a proposed decision that has not yet been enacted.
agenda_briefs.action_required: No action required from business owners at this time — watch for the council decision.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.8.b
Interagency Agreement Amendment with Contra Costa County Health Services for Emergency Medical Services Program
•  ADOPT a resolution to: (1) APPROVE and AUTHORIZE the city manager, or designee to execute an amendment to the agreement with Contra Costa County Health Services for Emergency Medical Services Program, extending the term of the current interagency agreement through June 30, 2027; and (2) ACCEPT and APPROPRIATE funds in an amount not to exceed an additional $229,462 in Measure H funding from the Contra Costa County Health Services, bringing the total contract payment limit to $458,924 – Fire Department (Chief Aaron Osorio 510-307-8021/Deputy Chief Rico Rincon 510-307-8041).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond council to designate officials for disaster aid applications
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a resolution that would authorize specific officials to apply for state and federal disaster assistance. If approved, the Fire Department would be able to submit applications to the California Governor’s Office of Emergency Services for financial help under the California Disaster Assistance Act and the federal Robert T. Stafford Disaster Relief and Emergency Assistance Act. This is a proposed step to prepare for future disasters — it has not been approved yet.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.8.a
Designating Officials Authorized to Execute and Submit Application for Federal and State Financial Disaster Assistance
•  ADOPT a resolution designating officials that are authorized to execute and submit applications to the State of California Governor’s Office of Emergency Services for state financial assistance under the California Disaster Assistance Act and the federal Robert T. Stafford Disaster Relief and Emergency Assistance Act – Fire Department (Shasa Curl 510-620-6512/Emily Combs 510-620-6740/Chief Aaron Osorio 510-307-8021).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond Donation Acceptance Policy up for council vote
agenda_briefs.summary: Richmond City Council is set to vote on a proposed Donation Acceptance Policy. If approved, this policy would create formal rules for how the city accepts donations from individuals, businesses, or organizations. The policy is meant to ensure donations are handled transparently and consistently. Council will consider adopting the policy at an upcoming meeting — it has not been approved yet.
agenda_briefs.action_required: No action required from business owners at this time. Watch for the council decision — the policy could affect future donation opportunities with the city.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.7.c
Donation Acceptance Policy
•  ADOPT a resolution approving the City of Richmond Donation Acceptance Policy – Finance Department (Emily Combs/Jerry Chak 510-620-6569).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond pension tax override rate set at 0.14% for 2026-27
agenda_briefs.summary: Richmond City Council is set to vote on a proposed ordinance that would set the tax rate for the Pension Tax Override Fund at 0.14% for the 2026-27 fiscal year. This is a first reading of the ordinance, meaning it has been introduced but not yet approved. If passed, this rate would apply to property taxes to fund pension obligations for city employees. The Finance Department is recommending this rate.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this could affect your property tax bill if you own a business in Richmond.

key_dates.label: Richmond pension tax override ordinance first reading

agenda_briefs.industries: Professional Services, Retail, Landlord & Property Mgmt
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.7.b
Setting Tax Rate for the Pension Tax Override Fund for Fiscal Year 2026-27
•  INTRODUCE an ordinance (first reading) setting the tax rate for the Tax Override Pension Fund for Fiscal Year 2026-2027 at 0.14 percent – Finance Department (Emily Combs/Mubeen Qader/ Antonio Banuelos 510-620-6741).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond Council to vote on writing off $535,499 in uncollected debts
agenda_briefs.summary: Richmond Council is set to vote on a proposal to write off $535,499.12 in uncollectible accounts receivable. This means the city would officially give up trying to collect this money from people or businesses that owe it. The money comes from various city fees, fines, or charges that have gone unpaid for a long time and are now considered unlikely to ever be paid. If approved, this would clear these old debts from the city's books. The decision has not been made yet — Council will consider and vote on the resolution at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: No action required from business owners at this time. Watch for the council decision — this does not affect current bills or taxes.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.7.a
Approval of Write-Off of Uncollectible Accounts Receivables
•  ADOPT a resolution authorizing the write-off of delinquent and/or uncollectible accounts receivable in the amount of $535,499.12 – Finance Department (Emily Combs/Mubeen Qader/Antonio Banuelos 510-620-6741).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond council to vote on $60K facade grant boost
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider approving an extra $60,000 for the Small Business Beautification Facade Improvement Program, bringing the total contract with Renaissance Entrepreneurship Center to $400,000. If approved, the program would also be extended through June 30, 2027. This program helps small businesses pay for exterior building improvements like new signs, paint, or storefront repairs. The decision is up for a vote at an upcoming council meeting — it has not been approved yet.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if approved, this program could help pay for your storefront upgrades. Contact the Economic Development Department to learn how to apply.

key_dates.label: Council vote on facade grant program extension

agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.6.b
Contract Amendment with Renaissance Entrepreneurship Center for Small Business Beautification Pilot Program
•  APPROVE the first amendment to the contract with Renaissance Entrepreneurship Center to increase the total contract amount by $60,000 for a revised amount not to exceed $400,000, extend the term through June 30, 2027, and support payment administration for eligible businesses participating in the Small Business Beautification Facade Improvement Program - Economic Development Department (Jesson de Leon 510-412-2023/Junne Garcia 510-620-6524/Nannette Beacham 510-620-1306).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Downtown Richmond property assessment levy for 2026-2027
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a resolution that would allow the City of Richmond to collect a special levy (a mandatory fee) from property owners in the Downtown Richmond Property and Business Improvement District. If approved, this levy would be added to the regular property tax bills for the 2026-2027 fiscal year. The money would be used to fund improvements and services in the downtown area.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council vote — if approved, this levy will appear on your property tax bill.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.6.a
Downtown Richmond Property and Business Improvement District Fiscal Year 2026–2027 Assessment Levy
•  ADOPT a resolution authorizing the City of Richmond to levy assessments on property owners subject to the Downtown Richmond Property and Business Improvement District through the County of Contra Costa property tax rolls for Fiscal Year 2026–2027 – Economic Development Department (Jesson de Leon 510-412-2023/Antonio Banuelos 510-620-6741/Junne Garcia 510-620-6524/Nannette Beacham 510-620-1306).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond council to accept $35,000 Chevron grant for street outreach
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a resolution to accept and appropriate a $35,000 grant from the Chevron Community Engagement Foundation. The money would go to the Office of Neighborhood Safety (ONS) to fund street outreach and mobile crisis response. This is a proposed decision — it has not been approved yet and is up for a vote at an upcoming council meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council meeting date — if you work in neighborhood safety or community outreach, this funding could affect your programs.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.5.a
Grant Award from Chevron Community Engagement Foundation
•  ADOPT a resolution to ACCEPT and APPROPRIATE a $35,000 grant award from the Chevron Community Engagement Foundation for the Office of Neighborhood Safety (ONS) to Enhance Street Outreach/Mobile Crises Response – Community Services Department (LaShonda White/Sam Vaughn 510-620-5404).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: $50,000 contract for housing program budget support
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider approving a three-year, on-call contract with Makanai, LLC for budget and reporting support related to past housing programs. The contract would be worth up to $50,000 and could be extended by one additional year. This is a proposed decision that has not yet been approved.
agenda_briefs.action_required: No action needed from business owners at this time — this is an internal administrative contract.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.4.b
Contract with Makanai, LLC for Budget and Reporting Support for Housing Programs
•  APPROVE an on-call contract with Makanai, LLC to provide budget and reporting support related to past housing programs in an amount not to exceed $50,000, for a three-year term with a term expiration of June 30, 2029, with one one-year extension option – Community Development Department (Lina Velasco/Jesus Morales 510-620-6706).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond council to vote on $300K hearing officer contracts
agenda_briefs.summary: Richmond Council is set to vote on a proposal to award on-call contracts to two law firms — Nastich Law and Robert M. Snider, Attorney at Law — to serve as Administrative Hearing Officers. These officers act like independent judges who hear disputes about local bylaws (for example, property or business licence issues). If approved, each firm could be paid up to $300,000 over a three-year term. The decision has not been made yet; council will consider it at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if approved, these contracts could affect how your business licence or property disputes are heard.
agenda_briefs.industries: Professional Services, Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.4.a
On-Call Contracts with Nastich Law and Robert M. Snider, Attorney at Law for Administrative Hearing Officer Services
•  ADOPT a resolution authorizing on-call contracts with Nastich Law and Robert M. Snider, Attorney at Law, to provide Administrative Hearing Officer services, for an amount not to exceed $300,000 per firm, for a three-year term - Community Development Department (Lina Velasco/ Eva Mann 510-621-1283).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond Council to vote on fee waiver for WCCUSD Latino/x graduation
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a proposal to waive all fees and staffing costs for the West Contra Costa Unified School District's Latino/x Graduation Ceremony at the Richmond Memorial Auditorium in 2026. If approved, the city manager would be authorized to spend city resources — including overtime for police, fire, public works, and other departments — to support the event. This is a proposed decision and has not yet been approved.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Contact Councilmember Claudia Jimenez (510-620-6565) to express support or concerns before the council vote.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.3.a
Waiver of Auditorium Fees, Staffing Costs, and Other Associated Costs to Support the West Contra Costa Unified School District Latino/x Graduation at the Richmond Memorial Auditorium and Convention Center
•  REQUEST the city manager issue fee waivers to West Contra Costa Unified School District for the use of the Richmond Memorial Auditorium in 2026 for the Latino/x Graduation Ceremony or Ceremonies, and AUTHORIZE the city manager or their designee to expend City resources to support the graduation, which may include overtime from Police, Fire, Public Works, Community Services, and other departments as required  - Councilmember Claudia Jimenez (510-620-6565).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond council to vote on $110,000 legal research contract
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed contract with Thomson Reuters for legal research subscription services. If approved, the contract would cost up to $110,000 over three years, from July 1, 2026, to June 30, 2029. The contract would provide the City Attorney's Office with access to Westlaw and CoCounsel Legal, which are online tools lawyers use to research laws and court cases. The city manager would be authorized to sign the final contract, with minor changes allowed as long as the City Attorney's Office agrees. This is a proposed decision — it has not been approved yet.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.1.c
Contract with Thomson Reuters Enterprise for Legal Research Subscription Services
•  APPROVE a contract with Thomson Reuters for legal research subscription services for Westlaw and CoCounsel Legal in an amount not to exceed $110,000 for the term of July 1, 2026, through June 30, 2029, subject to non-substantive revisions approved by the City Attorney’s Office; and AUTHORIZE the city manager or their designee to execute the contract – City Attorney’s Office (Christopher Dykzeul 510-620-6507/Shannon Moore 510-620-6509).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond Council to vote on $150K personnel investigation contract
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider approving a legal services agreement with Ellis Investigations to conduct confidential personnel investigations for the City of Richmond. The proposed contract would run from July 1, 2026, through June 30, 2029, for a total amount not to exceed $150,000. This is a proposed decision — it has not been approved yet and will be voted on at an upcoming council meeting.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.1.b
Legal Services Agreement with Ellis Investigations for Confidential Personnel Investigations
•  APPROVE a legal services agreement with Ellis Investigations to conduct confidential personnel investigations for a term of July 1, 2026, through June 30, 2029, for a total amount not to exceed $150,000 – City Attorney’s Office/Human Resources Department (Shannon Moore/Nickie Mastay/Sharrone Taylor 510-620-6509).

agenda_briefs.title: Council to vote on $75,000 increase for Winehaven litigation legal fees
agenda_briefs.summary: Richmond City Council is set to vote on a proposed fourth amendment to a legal services agreement with the law firm Nevers, Palazzo, Packard, Wildermuth & Wynner, PC. If approved, the amendment would increase the total contract amount by $75,000 — from $375,000 to $450,000 — to cover legal fees related to the ongoing Winehaven Legacy litigation. The term would also be extended through December 31, 2027. The additional $75,000 would come from the city's General Fund. This is a proposed decision and has not yet been approved.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.1.a
Legal Services Agreement No. 4 with Nevers, Palazzo, Packard, Wildermuth & Wynner, PC
•  APPROVE a fourth amendment to the legal services agreement with Nevers, Palazzo, Packard, Wildermuth & Wynner, PC, for legal representation related to the Winehaven Legacy litigation and related matters, to increase compensation by $75,000 for a total contract amount not to exceed $450,000, extend the term through December 31, 2027, and APPROPRIATE $75,000 from the General Fund to cover associated legal fees. – City Attorney’s Office (Kimberly Chin 510-620-6709/Shannon Moore 510-620-6505).
